title: World War II
subtitle: A Global Conflict and Its Lasting Impact

overview.title: Understanding World War II
overview.description: World War II was a global conflict fought between 1939 and 1945 that reshaped nations, societies, and international relations.

sections.title: Global Scale
sections.text: The war involved many of the world’s nations across Europe, Asia, Africa, and the Pacific.

sections.title: Human Cost
sections.text: World War II caused unprecedented destruction and loss of life, including the Holocaust.

sections.title: Legacy
sections.text: The war led to major political changes and the creation of international organisations such as the United Nations.

clusters.title: Causes of the War
clusters.description: Factors that led to the outbreak of World War II.

nodes.title: Treaty of Versailles
nodes.subtitle: Aftermath of World War I
nodes.description: Harsh terms imposed on Germany created resentment and instability.

content.text: The treaty punished Germany with territorial losses, military limits, and heavy reparations.

nodes.title: Rise of Dictators
nodes.subtitle: Authoritarian leaders
nodes.description: Leaders like Hitler, Mussolini, and Tojo pursued aggressive expansion.

content.text: These leaders used nationalism and propaganda to gain support.

nodes.title: Failure of Appeasement
nodes.subtitle: Weak international response
nodes.description: Western powers failed to stop early aggression.

content.text: The Axis aimed to dominate Europe and Asia.

nodes.title: Allied Powers
nodes.subtitle: UK, USA, USSR and others
nodes.description: Countries united to defeat the Axis.

content.text: Allies combined military and industrial strength.

nodes.title: Australia’s Role
nodes.subtitle: Fighting overseas
nodes.description: Australia supported Britain and later the USA.

content.text: Australian troops fought in Europe, North Africa, and the Pacific.

clusters.title: Key Events and Turning Points
clusters.description: Events that shaped the outcome of the war.

items.title: Poland Invaded
items.text: Britain and France declared war on Germany.

nodes.title: Pearl Harbor
nodes.subtitle: USA enters war
nodes.description: Japanese attack on a US naval base.

content.text: The attack led to the United States joining the war.

nodes.title: D-Day
nodes.subtitle: Allied invasion
nodes.description: The liberation of Western Europe began.

content.text: Allied forces landed in Normandy on 6 June 1944.

clusters.title: The Holocaust
clusters.description: Systematic persecution and genocide by Nazi Germany.

nodes.title: Nazi Ideology
nodes.subtitle: Racism and antisemitism
nodes.description: Belief in racial superiority drove persecution.

content.text: Jews and other groups were blamed for Germany’s problems.

nodes.title: Concentration Camps
nodes.subtitle: Imprisonment and forced labour
nodes.description: Millions were detained under brutal conditions.

nodes.title: Genocide
nodes.subtitle: Mass murder
nodes.description: Approximately six million Jews were killed.

clusters.title: End of the War and Legacy
clusters.description: How the war ended and its consequences.

content.text: Berlin fell to Soviet forces in May 1945.
These bombings caused massive destruction and loss of life.

nodes.title: Post-war World
nodes.subtitle: Rebuilding and change
nodes.description: New global systems aimed to prevent future wars.

content.text: The United Nations was formed to promote peace.
